metadata.summary: Open Source Combatlog Reader for Star Trek Online.

documentation:
# OSCR
Open Source Combatlog Reader for Star Trek Online. This repository contains the parser itself. It is intended to compile this into a package and make it accessible via PyPi.

classes.name: OSCR.liveparser.LiveParser

methods.name: OSCR.liveparser.LiveParser.__init__
documentation:
Creates LiveParser Instance.

Parameters:
\- :param log_path: path to combatlog that is being analyzed
\- :param start_callback: function that is called immediately before the live parser starts
to analyze the logfile initiated by the LiveParser.start() function; no arguments will be
passed
\- :param update_callback: function that is called once every second when the parser is
running; it will be passed a dictionary with the data acquired in the last second as
positional argument
\- :param settings: contains settings

> - "seconds_between_combats": number of inactive seconds after which a new engagement
>
> resets the collected data

methods.name: OSCR.liveparser.LiveParser.__del__
documentation: Stops all active threads when the object is garbage-collected.

methods.name: OSCR.liveparser.LiveParser.start
documentation: Initiates the parsing process.

methods.name: OSCR.liveparser.LiveParser.stop
documentation:
Terminates the parsing process. Already running threads might perform one more data
iteration before the process ends.

methods.name: OSCR.liveparser.LiveParser.update_data
documentation: Refines the data and executes the update_callback. Runs once per second when active.

methods.name: OSCR.liveparser.LiveParser.analyze
documentation:
Analyzes the log continuously until LiveParser.stop() is called. Clears existing data first
when called.
OSCR's realtime parser

methods.name: OSCR.main.OSCR.analyzed_combats
documentation: Contains tuple with available combats.

methods.name: OSCR.main.OSCR.active_combat
documentation: Combat currently active (selected).

methods.name: OSCR.main.OSCR.navigation_up
documentation: Indicates whether newer combats are available, but not yet analyzed.

methods.name: OSCR.main.OSCR.navigation_down
documentation: Indicates whether older combats are available, but not yet analyzed.

methods.name: OSCR.main.OSCR.analyze_log_file
documentation:
Analyzes the combat at self.log_path and replaces self.combats with the newly parsed
combats.

Parameters:
\- :param total_combats: holds the number of combats that should be in self.combats after
the method is finished.
\- :param extend: extends the list of current combats to match the number of total_combats
by analyzing excess_log_lines
\- :param log_path: specify log path different from self.log_path to be analyzed. Has no
effect when parameter extend is True

methods.name: OSCR.main.OSCR.analyze_massive_log_file
documentation:
Analyzes the combat at self.log_path and replaces self.combats with the newly parsed
combats. Used to analyze log files larger than around 500000 lines. Wraps around
self.analyze_log_file.

Parameters:
\- :param total_combats: holds the number of combats that should be in self.combats after
the method is finished.

methods.name: OSCR.main.OSCR.navigate_log
documentation:
Analyzes earlier combats when direction is "down"; loads earlier templog file if current
file is exhausted; loads later combatlog file if direction is "up".

Parameters:
\- :param direction: "down" and "up"

```
return

True when logfile was changed; False otherwise
```

methods.name: OSCR.main.OSCR.full_combat_analysis
documentation: Analyzes combat
def shallow_combat_analysis(self, combat_num: int) -> tuple[list, ...]:
Analyzes combat from currently available combats in self.combat.
Parameters:
- :param combat_num: index of the combat in self.combats
:return: tuple containing the overview table, DPS graph data and DMG graph data
try:
combat = self.combats[combat_num]
combat.analyze_shallow(graph_resolution=self._settings['graph_resolution'])
self.combats_pointer = combat_num
except IndexError:
raise AttributeError(
f'Combat #{combat_num} you are trying to analyze has not been isolated yet. '
f'Number of isolated combats: {len(self.combats)} -- Use '
'OSCR.analyze_log_file() with appropriate arguments first.')

methods.name: OSCR.main.OSCR.export_combat
documentation:
Exports combat to new logfile

Parameters:
\- :param combat_num: index of the combat in self.combats
\- :param path: path to export the log to, will overwrite existing files

classes.name: OSCR.combat.Combat

methods.name: OSCR.combat.Combat.analyze_last_line
documentation: Analyze the last line and try and detect the map and difficulty

methods.name: OSCR.combat.Combat.analyze_shallow
documentation:
Do a shallow combat analysis
The goal of the shallow combat analysis is to get as much data
as we can in a single iteration of the log data. This includes building
damage over time graphs so that the log does not need to be iterated
over again.

methods.name: OSCR.combat.Combat.analyze_players
documentation: Analyze players to determine time-based metrics such as DPS.

methods.name: OSCR.combat.Combat.analyze_critters
documentation:
Analyze map entities Computers to determine:
    - The map type
    - The difficulty of the map

If new results are obtained, this overrides the values previously set
if detect_line() was called during the creation of the Combat object.

The algorithm starts broad and then narrows in if additional detections
are necessary. The order in which they are processed:
    - Entity Counts
    - Entity Hull Damage Taken

On maps such as Infected Space Entity Hull Damage Taken
(and later steps) do not need to be provided if Entity Counts is
sufficient in determinning map valididty.

Assumes that map and difficulty have already been set with detect_line.

functions.name: OSCR.iofunc.split_log_by_combat
documentation:
Splits off a number of combats from logfile and saves them to target path.

Parameters:
\- :param log_path: path to existing logfile
\- :param target_path: path to target file; will be overwritten if already existing!
\- :param first_num: first combat to include in the new file
\- :param last_num: last combat to include in the new file
\- :param combat_distance: seconds between combats
\- :param excluded_event_ids: combats starting with an event id from this list will be ignored

functions.name: OSCR.iofunc.save_log
documentation:
Saves lines to new file.

Parameters:
\- :param path: path to log file
\- :param lines: list of lines to write to file; individual lines may be of type str or LogLine
\- :param overwrite: overwrites existing file when set to True

functions.name: OSCR.iofunc.sanitize_file_name
documentation:
Converts txt to a valid filename.

Parameters:
\- :param txt: The path to convert.
\- :param chr_set:

> - 'printable': Any printable character except those disallowed on Windows/nix.
>
> - 'extended': 'printable' + extended ASCII character codes 128-255
>
> - 'universal': For almost *any* file system.

Contains a single row of data in the analysis table. Unpacks into: (name, handle, total_damage,
max_one_hit, kills, total_attacks, misses, crit_num, flank_num, total_shield_damage,
total_hull_damage, total_base_damage, combat_time, hull_attacks, shield_attacks)

functions.name: OSCR.parser.merge_single_lines
documentation:
Eliminates one level of depth if needed by merging lines that only have a single child. For
example:

v Quantum Mines
: v Quantum Mine 34

  : \> Mine Explosion

  v Quantum Mine 25

  : \> Mine Explosion

becomes:

v Quantum Mines - Mine Explosion

> \> Quantum Mine 34
>
> \> Quantum Mine 25

Parameters:
\- :param tree_model: tree model to analyze and improve
